coredata.dc:title: Full-spectrum responsive photoelectrochemical immunoassay based on β-In2S3@carbon dot nanoflowers
coredata.dc:creator: Yu, Zhonghua
Huang, Lingting
Chen, Jialun
Tang, Ying
Xia, Biyun
Tang, Dianping
coredata.dc:description: This work designed a new photoelectrochemical (PEC) immunoassay for the ultrasensitive detection of alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) based on the flower-like β-In2S3@carbon dots (CDs). The photocurrent of the as-prepared heterogeneous nanostructure was enhanced significantly under ultraviolet (UV), visible (Vis) and near-infrared (NIR) irradiation thanks to the more generation of carriers and less electron-hole pairs recombination with CDs. The three-layer sandwich-like immunocomplex would be formed upon the target AFP existed, the ascorbic acid 2-phosphate (AA2P) was subsequently transformed into ascorbic acid (AA) owing to the catalysis of alkaline phosphatase-conjugated rabbit polyclonal anti-human AFP (ALP-Ab2). Without additional applied voltage, the photocurrent of the β-In2S3@CDs-modified electrode was positively correlated with the increasing AA level in the detection cell. Under optimal conditions, the developed PEC immunosensing platform exhibited good reproducibility and high sensitivity for the determination of target AFP within a dynamic working range from 0.5 to 50 ng mL−1 at a detection limit of 37.9 pg mL−1. In addition, the method accuracy relative to human AFP ELISA kit was evaluated by analyzing human serum specimens, and a good match between two methods was obtained. Such the increasing photocurrent through full-spectrum responsive mode gives a new horizon to develop highly sensitive PEC immunoassay in early clinical diagnosis by monitoring disease-related biomarkers.
coredata.dcterms:subject: β-In2S3
Carbon dots
Full-spectrum response
Photoelectrochemical immunosensor

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Synthesis of flower-like β-In2S3@CDs nanoparticle with hierarchical 3D architectures
Feasibility evaluation of PEC sensing system
Analytical performance of β-In2S3@CDs-based PEC immunosensor toward target AFP
Monitoring of human serum samples and evaluation of method accuracy

xocs:funding.xocs:funding-agency: National Natural Science Foundation of China

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (A) TEM image, (B) SEM image, and (C, D) HRTEM images of flower-like β-In2S3@CDs; (E) XRD pattern of samples (β-In2S3@CDs and pure β-In2S3); (F) HAADF-STEM image and element mapping for In, S, and C of β-In2S3@CDs.

(A) UV–vis–NIR diffuse reflectance spectra of pure β-In2S3 and β-In2S3@CDs (insert: the corresponding plot of (hυ)1/2 vs. hυ.); (B) upconverted PL spectra of CDs under different excitation wavelengths; (C) photocurrent of pure β-In2S3 and β-In2S3@CDs under UV, VIS and NIR irradiation; (D) photocurrent responses of β-In2S3@CDs electrode under Xe lamp in Na2SO4 solution (0.1 M) in the (a) absence and (b) presence of 5.0 ng mL−1 target AFP.

Effects of (A) the doping concentration of CDs, (B) pH of the solution, (C) the concentrations of AA2P in the substrate, (D) the enzymolysis time of ALP on the photocurrent of the β-In2S3@CDs electrode in Tris-HCl buffer (5.0 ng mL−1 AFP used in this case).

(A) Photocurrents of the β-In2S3@CDs electrode in Tris-HCl buffer (pH 9.0) containing 15 mM AA2P after reaction Of immunoassay system with different-concentration target AFP standards; (B) calibration curve, (C) the stability of the PEC immunoassay system; (D) the specificity of the PEC immunoassay against 5.0 ng mL−1 AFP, 50 ng mL−1 CEA, 50 ng mL−1 PSA, 50 ng mL−1 CA 125, and 50 ng mL−1 CA 13–5.
Schematic illustration of the photoelectrochemical immunosensing system for the detection of alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) on β-In2S3@CDs photoelectrode by coupling with enzyme immunoassay format.
Comparison of analytical results of β-In2S3@CDs-based PEC immunoassay and AFP ELISA kit for human serum specimens.

row.entry: method; conc. (mean ± SD, ng mL−1, n = 3)

head.ce:title: Full-spectrum responsive photoelectrochemical immunoassay based on β-In2S3@carbon dot nanoflowers

Chongqing University of Arts and Sciences

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: College of Pharmacy/International Academy of Targeted Therapeutics and Innovation, Chongqing University of Arts and Sciences, Chongqing 402160, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: This work designed a new photoelectrochemical (PEC) immunoassay for the ultrasensitive detection of alpha-fetoprotein (AFP) based on the flower-like β-In2S3@carbon dots (CDs). The photocurrent of the as-prepared heterogeneous nanostructure was enhanced significantly under ultraviolet (UV), visible (Vis) and near-infrared (NIR) irradiation thanks to the more generation of carriers and less electron-hole pairs recombination with CDs. The three-layer sandwich-like immunocomplex would be formed upon the target AFP existed, the ascorbic acid 2-phosphate (AA2P) was subsequently transformed into ascorbic acid (AA) owing to the catalysis of alkaline phosphatase-conjugated rabbit polyclonal anti-human AFP (ALP-Ab2). Without additional applied voltage, the photocurrent of the β-In2S3@CDs-modified electrode was positively correlated with the increasing AA level in the detection cell. Under optimal conditions, the developed PEC immunosensing platform exhibited good reproducibility and high sensitivity for the determination of target AFP within a dynamic working range from 0.5 to 50 ng mL−1 at a detection limit of 37.9 pg mL−1. In addition, the method accuracy relative to human AFP ELISA kit was evaluated by analyzing human serum specimens, and a good match between two methods was obtained. Such the increasing photocurrent through full-spectrum responsive mode gives a new horizon to develop highly sensitive PEC immunoassay in early clinical diagnosis by monitoring disease-related biomarkers.

ce:section.ce:para: Photoelectrochemical (PEC) process, as a novel detection technique, has attracted broad attention in the field of biosensor [1–3]. The semiconductor on the photoelectrode plays the role of the energy converter, captures the light source and separates the photogenerated electron-hole pairs, then transfers the charge carrier to the electrode surface [4]. Using two forms of signal for excitation and detection endows the PEC platform with higher sensitivity and lower LOD because of the reduced background noise, meanwhile, it naturally inherits the merits of the electrochemical analysis such as low cost, accessible apparatus and less time-consuming [5]. Consequently, the properties of the photoactive materials are essential to the analytical performance of the PEC biosensor and the amplification of the photoelectronic signal is critical to improving the detection sensitivity [6]. To date, our group has devoted continuously to the PEC study and explores various kinds of strategies to regulate the production and transfer pathways of photogenerated carriers for ameliorating the analytical performance [7]. Reducing the recombination of the electron-hole pairs enables more carriers transferred to the electrode surface, which is an effective means to enhance the photocurrent. To achieve this purpose, we exploited many approaches such as using the artificial Z-scheme systems which imitate natural photosynthesis through the “Z”-type electron-transfer process to facilitate charge separation [8–10], coupling semiconductors with the noble-metal nanoparticles on account of the local surface plasmon resonance (LSPR) [9,11]. But there was one issue that stood out to us is the utilized optical spectrum region of the photoactive semiconductor is narrow, they only can be employed in the limited region like ultraviolet–visible light. We attempted to combine the upconversion nanoparticles with the semiconductor like TiO2 [12], Ag2S [13], and CdS [14] use the near-infrared (NIR) to ultraviolet or visible strategy and get the ideal analytical performances, but it just changed the excitation wavelength, didn’t broaden the utilized spectrum range radically. Therefore, further research is necessary to take good advantage of the overall luminous energy that increases the number of photogenerated carriers to enhance the sensitivity of the PEC system.

β-In2S3, a typical III-VI group chalcogenide, n-type semiconductor, has favorable and promising properties of narrow bandgap (2.0–2.3 eV), high photosensitivity and photoconductivity, stable chemical and physical characteristics and low toxicity [15–18]. Gao et al. [19] utilize tetragonal In2S3 nanoparticles as broadband spectrum photocatalysis owing to its high photodegradation abilities under broad-spectral. Besides, due to the similar optoelectronic performance as CdS but owning the low toxicity characteristics, β-In2S3 is becoming a substitution of CdS in the solar cell [20]. The applications of CDs in photocatalysts [21], drug delivery [22], bioimaging, and sensors [23] have drawn intense attention owing to their ease of synthesis, non-toxicity, unique structure, superior optical properties (strong size and excitation wavelength-dependent photoluminescence (PL), upconversion PL capacity), outstanding electron-transfer efficiency, and tunable light-harvesting range. Li et al. [24] first reported novel a photocatalyst of CDs through a one-step alkali metal-assisted electrochemical method with size-dependent PL and upconversion PL. Kang’s group further exploited a series of CD-based photocatalysts coupled with Fe2O3 [25], ZnO [26], Ag3PO4 [27] or Cu2O [28], and evaluated their photocatalytic degradation performance under VIS- or NIR-driven irradiation.

Inspired by the above, we synthesize a novel flower-like β-In2S3@CDs nanoparticle, applying it into the photoelectrochemical study to realize the full-spectrum utilization and enhance the sensitivity. Under high-temperature calcination, the uniform flower-like structure was obtained, making composite possess a higher specific surface area and capture the sacrificial agent easily to improve the analytical sensitivity [29]. The decorated CDs plays an important role in the enhancement of photocurrent, it is a good conductor, acts as an electron reservoir and reduce the recombination of the electron-hole pairs [21]. Moreover, CDs have the upconversion fluorescence emission ability which transforms the long-wavelength light source into the relatively high energy light, endowing the utilization of the NIR-light, bordering the absorption spectrum [30].

Alpha-fetoprotein (AFP, a serum biomarker of primary liver cancer) is closely related to the occurrence of liver cancer and various tumors and is mainly used for the diagnosis and therapeutic effect monitoring of primary liver cancer [31,32]. Herein we develop a new sensitive and feasible PEC immunosensing platform for AFP (as a model analyte) detection based on β-In2S3@CDs (Scheme 1 ). The sandwich-like immunocomplex will be formed in the presence of AFP, and hydrolysis the substrate AA2P into AA in the microplate. Consequently, the solution was transferred into the detection cell, and the photocurrent was tested in the glare of a xenon lamp. Because of the good reducing property of AA, it can play the role of photoanode sacrificial agent. As a result, the photocurrent will enlarge with the increment of AA concentration to achieve the purpose of target AFP detection.

Alpha-fetoprotein standards, mouse monoclonal anti-human AFP (Ab1), rabbit polyclonal anti-human AFP (Ab2) and alkaline phosphatase (ALP) were all supplied by Sangon Biotech. Co., Ltd (Shanghai, China). l-Ascorbic acid 2-phosphate sesquimagnesium salt hydrate (AA2P), albumin from bovine serum (BSA) and sodium carbonate (Na2CO3) were acquired from Sigma-Aldrich (Merck KGaA, Darmstadt, Germany). InCl3·4H2O and ethylenediamine were gotten from Aladdin (Shanghai, China). l-cysteine and citric acid (C6H8O7) were purchased from Sinopharm Chemical Reagent Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). All other chemicals were of analytical grade without further purification. Ultrapure water (18.2 MΩ cm−1) was prepared via a Millipore water purification system (Milli-Q, Merck KGaA, Germany). All buffers including the Tris-HCl buffer were the products of Sigma-Aldrich.

CDs were prepared through a hydrothermal approach. In brief, citric acid (1.0507 g) and ethylenediamine (0.45 g) were dissolved in ultrapure water (10 mL). Then the solution was transferred to a Teflon-lined stainless autoclave (30 mL) and heated at 180 °C for 5 h. Finally, the products were subjected to dialysis to obtain the CDs.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of flower-like β-In2S3@CDs nanoparticle with hierarchical 3D architectures
ce:section.ce:para: In a typical procedure, the β-In2S3@CDs hybrid materials were synthesized via a one-pot hydrothermal method, InCl3·4H2O (1.0 mmol), l-cysteine (3.0 mmol) and a certain amount of as-prepared CDs were dispersed in 80-mL ultrapure water. After stirring for 20 min, the pH of the solution was adjusted to 8 by adding NaOH (1.0 M) dropwise. Subsequently, the mixture was sealed in a Teflon-lined stainless steel autoclave (100 mL) and maintained at 180 °C for 10 h. The obtained products were washed with distilled water and absolute ethanol several times and then dried in vacuum at 60 °C overnight. Finally, the pre-synthesized samples were annealed in a tube furnace at 400 °C for 10 min under argon atmosphere. The heating rate for the tube furnace was 10 °C/min before the furnace temperature reached 400 °C.
ALP-Ab2 conjugates were prepared according to the previous research methods of our group [33]. The detailed description of preparation was supplied in the Supplementary data.

Initially, the 96-well microplate was coated with capture Ab1 at 4 °C overnight, then various concentrations of AFP standard/sample (50 μL) was injected into it and incubated for 30 min at 37 °C under shaking. After washing away the unbound antigen, 50 μL ALP-Ab2 (1:500 ratio dilution) was introduced and incubated for 30 min at 37 °C under shaking to form the structure of sandwich-like immunocomplex. Subsequently, the immunoenzyme conjugate was obtained after rinsed thoroughly with wash buffer and then the microwells were kept at 37 °C for 40 min in Tris-HCl buffer (40 mM, pH 9.0) containing 15-mM AA2P. Finally, the resulting solution was transferred into the detection cell for photoelectrochemical measurement which was carried out on an AutoLab Electrochemical Workstation (μAUTIII.FRA2.v, Eco Chemie, Netherlands) under the irradiation of a 500 W Xe lamp (Solar-500, NBET, Beijing, China) at a constant potential of 0 V with a conventional three-electrode system consisting of an β-In2S3@CDs-modified FTO (Please see the Supplementary data) working electrode (WE), a Pt-wire counter electrode (CE) and a saturated calomel electrode (SCE) as the reference electrode (RE). All determinations were measured at least three times.

The corresponding intrinsic morphologies and microstructures were characterized by using scanning electron microscopy (SEM: SIGMA HD, Germany) and transmission electron microscopy (TEM; Hitachi 7700, Japan). As shown in Fig. 1 A–B, the SEM and TEM of as-synthesized materials display a complete and uniform flower-like structure with well-arranged nanoflakes as we excepted and the diameter of β-In2S3@CDs is about 600–800 nm. The mono-structure before and after doping CDs was investigated (Fig. S1), with the decoration of CDs, the flower-like main structure does not change, but the arrangement of flakes becomes more compact. The high-resolution TEM (HRTEM; JEOL, JEMARM200FTH) was used to validate the formation of compounds Plenty of CDs can be found easily on the surface of β-In2S3 nanoflowers to form the heterostructure (Fig. 1C), and three different kinds of lattice fringes are clearly observed (Fig. 1D). The lattice fringe spacing with ca. 0.33 nm matched the (002) crystallographic plane of CDs, another two lattice fringe spacing of ca. 0.62 nm and ca. 0.32 nm corresponds to (111) and (311) planes of β-In2S3 (JCPDS No. 320456) [17,34].

The crystal structure of the obtained samples was examined by XRD (PANalytical X’Pert spectrometer, Netherlands). As shown in Fig. 1E, all of the diffraction peaks of samples located at 2θ = 14.3°, 27.4°, 28.7°, 33.2°, 43.6°, 47.7°, 56.5° and 59.4° are distinctly indexed to the (111), (311), (222), (400), (511), (440), (533) and (444) crystal planes, match well with the JCPDS No. 320456, indicating the cubic crystalline phase of β-In2S3 [35]. In addition, the crystalline structure changes almost nothing with the introduction CDs into the compound, which is probably owing to the similar lattice fringe spacings between (002) plane of CDs (3.25 Å) and (311) plane of β-In2S3 (3.23 Å) [34,36]. No other impure phases are observed of the XRD patterns, suggesting the high purity and well-crystallized of the as-prepared samples.

The energy dispersive spectrum (EDS) of β-In2S3@CDs (Fig. 1F) indicates that the as-prepared sample consists of uniformly distributed elements of C, In, and S elements. The above results suggest that CDs have been introduced to the β-In2S3 phase and the uniform dispersed heterostructure is formed successfully.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Feasibility evaluation of PEC sensing system
ce:section.ce:para: The main purpose of this work is to realize a full-spectrum response PEC immunosensor to enhance the photocurrent and improve the detection performance. As shown in Fig. 2 A, the optical absorption property was characterized by the UV–vis diffuse reflectance spectra (DRS, Tecan Infinite 200 Pro, 230–600 nm, Switzerland). The pure In2S3 sample exhibits a wide range of light absorption with an absorption edge of around 600 nm, while the absorption edge shows a significant redshift and the absorption intensities ranged from 610 to 1100 nm enhances greatly after the introduction of CQDs. In addition, the bandgap (Eg) of samples is narrowed from 2.21ev to 1.84ev after coupling with CDs (Fig. 2A, insert). The corresponding Eg was calculated via the formula according to the Kubelka-Munka of (αhυ)1/2 = A (hυ – Eg) for direct transition semiconductor, where α, h, ν, A and Eg represents the absorption coefficients, Planck’s constant, the light frequency, the constant, and the bandgap energy respectively [37]. As a result, we infer that the introduction of CDs can significantly extend the absorption range and reduce the bandgap. Besides the PL spectrum (Hitachi F-4600 FL spectrophotometer) of CDs under 600, 650, 700, 750, 800, 850, 900 nm excitation was studied, obvious upconvert Pl behaviors with emissions wavelength of 400–600 nm are observed (Fig. 2B). The pure β-In2S3 (bandgap ca. 2.2eV) can be excited to generate the electron-hole pairs with irradiation wavelength shorter than 600 nm. Therefore the upconvert PL capacity of CDs makes β-In2S3@CDs utilize more long-wavelength light, and further used as excitation wavelength light for the production of the photogenerated electron-hole pairs in β-In2S3.

To further demonstrate the degree of separation of the photogenerated electron-hole pairs after the addition of CDs into the complex, the photoelectrochemical experiments of pure β-In2S3 and β-In2S3@CDs electrode are conducted (Fig. 2C). As expected, the photocurrent of β-In2S3@CDs is enhanced significantly compared to pure In2S3 at all the UV, VIS and NIR light irradiation, the result indicates that with the CDs modified on the β-In2S3 render the charges separation more efficient and the lifetime of charge carriers longer. Here, the CDs act as a good electron acceptor and reservoir due to the conjugated π structure and excellent electrical conductivity.

Logically, a puzzling question arises to us is whether the photocurrent accompanies by the change of AA concentration which is triggered by the target level in the enzyme-linked immunoreaction. In this regard, we designed a set of control experiments that are conducted in the absence and presence of target AFP (5.0 ng mL−1) to verify whether the establishment of the PEC immunosensor is successful. As expected, the photocurrent increases obviously with the addition of AFP (5.0 ng mL−1) into the system (Fig. 2D).

Based on the above results, a proposed mechanism of the photocurrent generation is used to explain the enhancement of photocurrent (Fig. S2). Once the irradiation light wavelength is below 600 nm, the electrons on VB of β-In2S3 are excited to the CB and leaving h+ on VB and the electrons on the CB of β-In2S3 are liable to transfer to CDs. Another side, the CDs can absorb the light (wavelength greater than 600 nm) and emit shorter-wavelength light (300–530 nm) owing to the upconversion capacity, which in turn excites β-In2S3 to generate electron-hole pairs. And the generated AA by catalytic reaction of Ab2-ALP toward the substrate (AA2P) combines with holes on the VB of composite effectively to facilitate the separation of electron-hole pairs, thereby resulting in the amplification of the detection signal. All the above results demonstrated the feasibility of a full-spectrum response PEC immunosensor based on the β-In2S3@CDs for the target AFP detection.

Following the design, to achieve the extreme sensing performance of our PEC system based on the β-In2S3@CDs. The doping ratio of CDs in the composite, the substrate concentration (AA2P), PH, and enzymolysis time of enzyme-linked reaction have been investigated and optimized. The evaluation was based on the photocurrent difference of before and after changing variables with reaction using 5.0 ng mL−1 AFP as an example.

As mentioned above, CDs play a critical role to boost photocurrent, thus, the doping ratio of CDs is directly affiliated to the detection signal. A series of β-In2S3@CDs with different mass ratios of CDs from 0 to 5% were synthesized and taken into the comparative study. Fig. 3 A indicates that the photocurrent enlarges along with the increment of CDs concentration in a relatively low concentration, reaches the maximum at 4%, and decreases when the CDs doping ratio reaches 5%. Hence, we selected 4 wt% In2S3@CDs as the photoelectrode materials. The target-triggered AA produced by the enzyme-linked reaction is directly related to the photocurrent intensity. In this regard, we explored the optimal condition of ELISA. Due to PH has a considerable impact on the catalysis efficiency and activity of ALP, first, we investigated the influence of the pH values ranging from 7 to 10, and the maximum photocurrent was obtained at 9.0 (Fig. 3B). Thus, we controlled the PH of the solution is 9.0. Then, we monitored the effect of substrate concentration on photocurrent, as shown in Fig. 3C, the photocurrent increases with substrate concentration until the AA2P concentration is 15 mM. Consequently, using 15 mM AA2P as the optimal substrate concentration. Finally, the enzymolysis time of AA2P by Ab2-ALP was researched (Fig. 3D). The photocurrent initially increases with the enzymolysis time and reached a plateau after 40 min. To save assay time, 40 was selected as the enzymolysis time.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analytical performance of β-In2S3@CDs-based PEC immunosensor toward target AFP
ce:section.ce:para: Under optimal conditions, the analytical performance of β-In2S3@CDs-based PEC immunosensor was evaluated by quantitative detection of target AFP standards with different concentrations. With the introduction of the target analyte into the coated wells, the immunocomplex was formed and the conjugated ALP on Ab2 hydrolyzed AA2P into AA, then, resulting in the enhancement of the photocurrent. As expected, the photocurrent increases gradually with the increment of the AFP standards concentration (Fig. 4 A). In addition, the relationship between photocurrent and AFP concentration is a good linear in the dynamic range of 0.5–50 ng mL−1 with a LOD (S/N = 3) of 37.9 pg mL−1 (Fig. 4B). The linear regression equation could be fitted to y = 3.799 + 1.029 × lgC (ng ml−1), (R2 = 0.9912, n = 7), which could quantitatively calculate the exact concentration of AFP.

The reproducibility and stability of β-In2S3@CDs-based PEC immunoassay are important indicators for the analytical performance of the new PEC Platform. The relative standard deviations (RSDs) of intra- and inter-assays toward 0.5, 5.0, and 25 ng mL−1 (from low to high) target AFP sample was calculated to evaluate the reproducibility of PEC immunosensor. The RSDs were 1.3%, 5.0%, and 5.8% for intra-assay and 2.2%, 7.4%, and 8.6% for inter-assay, respectively (n = 3), demonstrating the good reproducibility of method. Meanwhile, the photocurrent of the β-In2S3@CDs electrode at on-off light irradiation circles was monitored to investigate the stability (Fig. 4C). The parallel curves were almost identical (RSD = 5.05%, n = 25), suggesting the excellent stability of readout. Due to no extra applied potential, the interference of electrocatalysis was eliminated. At the same time, the baseline of photocurrent was identical and near-zero at each test, thus reducing the error and improving the accuracy, reproducibility, and stability of the detection.

Finally, we systematically estimated the specificity of β-In2S3@CDs-based immunoassay by screening other common tumor biomarkers, such as PSA and CEA, under the optimal condition. As shown in Fig. 4D, the photocurrents of non-target analytes including PSA, CEA, CA 125, and CA 13–5 alone were approximately as same as the blank sample, and it increased notability when mixing with AFP specimen. The aforementioned results indicated that the β-In2S3@CDs-based PEC platform was high selectivity and exclusive for target AFP.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Monitoring of human serum samples and evaluation of method accuracy
ce:section.ce:para: To realize the future application of this PEC immunosensor in reality, we further carried the determination of human serum practical specimens (Mengchao Hepatobiliary Hospital of Fujian Medical University, Fuzhou, China). Additionally, the target AFP levels in actual samples were assayed by commercial available AFP ELISA kit (Cusabio Techn. Inc., Wuhan, China) for comparison. The method accuracy and relevance to kits were evaluated by using a Student’s t-test method [38,39]. As shown in Table 1 , all t exp values were below 2.78 at the 95% confidence level (t crit[0.05,4] = 2.78), indicating that the protocol performs excellently in the determination of AFP in biological fluids.

In summary, we adopted a strategy to broaden the absorption spectrum to enhance the photocurrent and designed a β-In2S3@CDs-based full-spectrum responsive PEC immunosensor for AFP detection. Compared with bare β-In2S3, utilizing β-In2S3@CDs as the photoelectrode materials could broaden the light responsive region from UV–visible to UV-NIR, thus increasing the photocurrent significantly under the illumination of the xenon lamp. The enhancing mechanism in the photocurrent is attributed to the decorated CDs that could not only take good advantage of long-wavelength light to generate more electron-hole pairs, but also separate the photogenerated electron-hole pairs of β-In2S3, making more electrons to transfer onto the photoelectrode. In the presence of target AFP, the immunocomplex was formed and hydrolyzes the substrate AA2P into AA in a microplate. The detection was based on the value of photocurrent increases with AA concentration. As a result of AA with a good reducing agent, it could capture the photogenerated holes feasibly, thus the value of photocurrent enlarged with increasing AA concentration and achieved the purpose of AFP detection. As expected, the PEC immunosensing platform exhibited high sensitivity, well reproducibility, long-term stability, and good selectivity. Importantly, this work can pave a new pathway to generate versatile PEC immunosensing platforms of other cancer biomarkers in a similar strategy of enhancing the photocurrent.

Zhonghua Yu: Investigation, Writing - original draft. Lingting Huang: Conceptualization, Writing - review & editing. Jialun Chen: Formal analysis. Ying Tang: Methodology, Supervision. Biyun Xia: Data curation, Software. Dianping Tang: Methodology, Supervision.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: Authors acknowledged financial support from the National Natural Science Foundation of China (21675029 & 201874022) and Health-Education Joint Research Project of Fujian Province (WKJ2016-2-15).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following is the Supplementary data to this article:
Multimedia component 1 Multimedia component 1

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.electacta.2019.135473.

I would like to declare on behalf of my co-authors that the work described was orginal research that has not been published previously and not under consideration for publication elsewhere, in whole or in part.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Photoelectrochemical bioanalysis: the state of the art

W. Zhao, J. Xu, H. Chen, Photoelectrochemical immunoassays, Anal. Chem. 90 (2018) 615-627.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Recent advances in photoelectrochemical sensing: from engineered photoactive materials to sensing devices and detection modes

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Perspective on signal amplification strategies and sensing protocols in photoelectrochemical immunoassay

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Photoelectrochemical biosensing of disease marker on p-type Cu-doped Zn0.3Cd0.7S based on RCA and exonuclease III amplification
